div.font:
Steps
(0) DNS: the workload resolves squid-proxy.egress.internal via the Route 53 Resolver and the Lattice-managed Private Hosted Zone, which returns an IP managed by VPC Lattice.
(1) The workload (HTTP_PROXY set to squid-proxy.egress.internal on 3128) makes an outbound request to the VPC Lattice IP over TCP 3128, entering its Service Network.
(2) The service network forwards the raw TCP flow to the egress resource gateway, which targets the internal NLB DNS name.
(3) The resource gateway forwards to the internal NLB on its :3128 listener.
(4) The NLB sends the connection to a healthy Squid Fargate task.
(5) Squid applies the FQDN allowlist — permitted domains proceed; non-allowlisted domains are denied.
(6) Allowed traffic egresses to the internet via the Egress VPC NAT Gateway. The response returns along the same path.

a.text: Text is not SVG - cannot display
